youtube_data.search_query: Granfondo Strade Bianche gran fondo cycling Siena

videos.title: Strade Bianche Gran Fondo: Cycling Perfection !
videos.description:
Last weekend I travelled to Siena, in Tuscany, Italy to be "on tour" with my good friends at Garda Bike Hotel, who had laid on some spectacular cycling and events over the course of the Strade Bianche Classics race - cumulating in me taking part in the Gran Fondo which follow's the lady's and men's race on the Saturday.  

It was my first time cycling in Tuscany, and wow!!! Simply wow!! Beautiful undulating countryside, with contrasting silky tarmac and plenty of iconic white gravel.  It was an exceptional event, within an exceptional weekend with exceptional company.

You'll see extracts of the UCI pro men's race plus my own effort navigating the Gran Fondo, Strade Bianche, including the iconic white gravel sectors and the famous final climb into Siena.  An experience I will cherish forever.  

Many thanks to Nicola, Davide, Alberto, Stefano and Daniel from Garda Bike Hotel - always consummate hosts, and always a ton of fun!! 

https://gardabikehotel.com

To translate the subtitles to your preferred language, please go to the settings cog on the video, and make sure subtitles are turned on.  Then select auto-translate, and then select the language you prefer.

Please leave those comments and hit that subscribe 

Thanks ever so much for all of your support! And whoever you are, what ever your do, please remember to LIVE THRIVE AND STAY HEALTHY ! 

Get Ignite by Fred V & Grafix, Amy J Pryce here https://lickd.lnk.to/ST7xB9ID!Bike%20Racing%20Without%20Mercy License ID: a4pjKM73x8E Get this and other songs for your next YouTube video at https://lickd.co

Follow my journey  
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/bikeracingwithoutmercy/
Strava: https://www.strava.com/athletes/BRWM

videos.title: Siena on the day of Granfondo Strade Bianche 2025
videos.description:
The beautiful city of Siena, Tuscany, in the day when Granfondo Strade Bianche 2025 is happening. Amazing atmosphere, cyclists everywhere and the magnificent Piazza del Campo full of life!

#siena #piazzadelcampo #tuscany #toscana #italy #italia #cycling #gfstradebianche #granfondo #stradebianche #bike #bikelife #summit #chasingsummits

videos.title: Strade Bianche 2025 - Racing the Gran Fondo and filming the PRO RACE
videos.description:
I made the pilgrimage to Siena, Italy to watch one of the most beloved bike races in the world, Strade Bianche. @Kask_Sport arranged for Alex and I to have a spot in one of the VIP cars so we were able to move around the course during the men's pro race and get some great shots. The day after we took to the white roads ourselves and rode the Gran Fondo which was almost identical to the pro women's race course, 138km long with 2000m of climbing. I raced it hard with the chest cam on, and Alex took his time with multiple cameras and a drone. We combined all the footage into this recap so enjoy the fastest humans in the world, some strong amateur racers, and those just trying to survive the course.

SUPPORT THE CHANNEL 
Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/safabrian
Merch: https://safa.link/apparel
Stickers: https://safa-brian.myshopify.com

videos.title: Goldilocks Gran Fondo ! Strade Bianche Fusing Road, Gravel, Speed & Exhilaration + Power Data
videos.description:
This year I said to myself there's no need to keep looking for the new best thing, or a bigger better challenge.  Just enjoy some of the events, activities and races you know you've loved in the past... so it was a 'no brainer' to sign back up for one of Europe's premier amateur events, Gran Fondo Strade Bianche.  Providing afficionado's a unique and exhilarating experience providing THE perfect fusion of punchy terrain, gravel, tarmac and speed !  The not too short and not too long, the Goldilocks Granfondo for sure!   

This video is different to my previous Strade Bianche Video, which had many camera angles and demonstrated the beauty of the event.  This one focuses on the rider perspective with full power overlay, so you can see where I pushed hard and where I conserved a little energy for a decent time.

As always Andiamo Cycling arranged a perfect 4 days, and I would also highlight that this is not a paid promotion, I pay full price and highly recommend them for this event and many others.  Their website link is below.

www.andiamocycling.com
www.gardabikehotel.com
  
To translate the subtitles to your preferred language, please go to the settings cog on the video, and make sure subtitles are turned on.  Then select auto-translate, and then select the language you prefer.

Please leave those comments and hit that subscribe 

Thanks ever so much for all of your support! And whoever you are, what ever your do, please remember to LIVE THRIVE AND STAY HEALTHY ! 

Follow my journey  
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/bikeracingwithoutmercy/
Strava: https://www.strava.com/athletes/BRWM
00:00 Introduction on the Start Line
00:47 Descent into Chaos !
03:47 1st Gravel Sector - Flowing
05:28 2nd Gravel Sector - Rapid
06:23 Chaos on 1st Climb !
11:23 Beauty, Speed & Strappatos
13:28 3rd Sector: Elevate then Accelerate
15:44 Rapid descent into 4-6th Sectors
17:38 STEELING Watts
19:00 7th Sector - STEEP STEEP STEEP
21:26 8th - The Koppenberg in Italia
23:22 9th - Gravel Finale - STEEPER STILL
25:26 Ascent into Siena: Fontebranda San Caterina
27:38 Finish Line, Medals & Peroni

videos.curation_reason: Recent detailed race experience with power data analysis and specific course insights.

quotes.text: I quickly discovered that it's much harder to climb on gravel you have what a revelation i probably should have realized that before i entered the event but at least here the gradients are forgiving.
Trust me it was seriously mad people were going ballistic it was like the start of a max effort crit race on zwift so hold the line let them come through and find yourself a nice group.

I don't know about you but I prefer to start all of my events on a descent with 6,000 other Riders many of them held bent on smashing today's time - only the Italians would commence a race on a descent that was just worse than a time before wild.

key_challenges.name: Sector One
key_challenges.description: First gravel sector providing a straight and flat two kilometer settler that requires a lot of concentration with people properly racing.

key_challenges.name: Sector Three Raddie
key_challenges.description: First big climb of the day with forgiving gradients but much harder to climb on gravel than expected, about 3 kilometers long.

key_challenges.name: Race Start
key_challenges.description: Mad ballistic start like a max effort crit race on Zwift with chaotic descent involving 6,000 riders, many bent on smashing personal times.

terrain_notes.text: Sector two features six kilometers of gently undulating and pleasingly fast pristine Tuscan gravel where 200 watts makes you feel like you're going at light speed.
The course is much spikier in real life than it appears on the profile, with relentless lumpy terrain, some of it proper steep.
Climbing on gravel is much harder than expected, requiring more effort than riders anticipate.

gear_mentions.text: Pinarella gravel bike with 52 millimeter tyres truly soaks up the lumps and bumps, enabling riders to move up nicely at 240 to 270 watts.
Thick thick tires recommended for improved handling and lower puncture risk, as reliability trumps weight savings in this event.

race_day_tips.text: Hold the line at the mad ballistic start, let riders come through and find yourself a nice group rather than getting caught up in the chaos.
Get to the sanctuary of an upward gradient quickly after the chaotic descent start to avoid crashes and find safer positioning.

additional_quotes.text: The start of all real life Grand fondos in Italia should replicate his favorite cycling game so by legal decree involving no bribe of any Senator let the chaos rip
Does life get any better than threading the eye of a needle through hundreds of riders up a gravel climb in Tuscany

rider_intel.search_text: The Granfondo Strade Bianche features 86 kilometers with 1600 meters of climbing across 10 white gravel sectors through Tuscan countryside. Riders describe a chaotic ballistic start resembling a max effort crit race, often beginning on a descent with 6,000 participants. The course includes notable sectors like the straight flat two-kilometer Sector One requiring concentration, six kilometers of fast pristine gravel in Sector Two where 200 watts feels like light speed, and the challenging Sector Three Raddie climb. The terrain is much spikier than profiles suggest, with relentless lumpy conditions and gravel climbing proving harder than expected. Riders recommend thick 52mm tires for handling and puncture resistance, emphasizing reliability over weight. Key strategy involves avoiding start chaos by finding a steady group and reaching upward gradients quickly for safer positioning.
